name: Select
summary: Fully-typed dropdown for single or `multiple` choice from `Select.Option` children; controlled via `value` + `onValueChange` and FormControl-aware — use it for compact pick-one(-or-many) lists, and prefer `MetroSelect` when options need a visual tile grid.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Canonical value change event (fires on selection).

props.name: width
props.description:
Explicit field width — number → px, or any CSS length. Overrides the
default `width:100%`.

props.name: label
props.description: Visual label rendered by the component or its wrapper. Accepts string or node.

props.name: helperText
props.description: Supplemental hint or validation content typically rendered under the control.

props.name: error
props.description: Marks the field invalid; components may adjust visuals and helper text color.

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: Stretches the field wrapper to occupy the full available width.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: Choose controlled vs uncontrolled deliberately: use `value` + `onValueChange` for controlled state; use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled, and handle commit-time actions in `onValueCommit`.

It fills its container's width by default (like TextField) and stays in its own column in a row; set an explicit `width` (number → px, or any CSS length) to constrain it, or `fullWidth` to grow in a row. (Pre-1.0 it was content-width, which let an open menu drift over a neighbouring field.)

Inside a `FormControl`, a form-wide `disabled` disables the trigger and a name-keyed `errors` entry marks it invalid (the Select's own `disabled`/`error` still win). On coarse pointers the trigger and option rows floor at ≥44px (24px under `compact`).
Keep option labels short, distinct, and ordered predictably (alphabetical or by frequency). Avoid duplicating values with different labels.
Use clear placeholders. Show a short prompt when no value is selected (e.g., ‘Choose one’). Don’t use real values as placeholders.
Keyboard & typeahead. Ensure focus and Arrow keys navigate options; enable first‑letter search for long lists.
Avoid deep nesting. If options have complex structure or long descriptions, consider a dedicated picker UI instead of Select.
Multiple selections. Prefer `MetroSelect` with visible chips when the number of selections should be immediately clear.
Performance. For very long lists, consider server‑side filtering or virtualized menus; keep menu height bounded.
Presets/tokens. Style via theme tokens and `preset` to maintain consistent control density and colours.
A11y. Ensure the trigger is labelled (via `label` or surrounding `FormControl`) and that the selected value is announced clearly.

Overlay behaviour. The open menu portals to the shared overlay root and registers on the overlay stack, so it escapes any `transform`/`overflow` clipping ancestor of the trigger. Inside a Modal, pressing Escape closes only the dropdown (the top-most layer); the Modal stays open.

Binding precedence (prop > form > internal). An explicit `value` prop always wins; otherwise a `name` inside a `FormControl` binds to the store (seed the key in `createFormStore(initial)` so the initial selection and `form.reset()` are honoured); otherwise the Select is uncontrolled. The binding is latched at mount — don't switch a Select between controlled and uncontrolled.

Change source. `onValueChange`/`onValueCommit` report a truthful `info.source`: `'pointer'` for a mouse selection and `'keyboard'` for keyboard activation (Enter/Space/arrow), not a blanket `'programmatic'`.

Option props. `Select.Option` forwards its own `className`, `style`, `id`, `data-*` (and arbitrary `<li>` attributes), plus `sx`/`preset`, onto the rendered option element. A custom `id` wins over the generated one and keeps `aria-activedescendant` in sync. Internal listbox semantics (`role`, `aria-selected`) always take precedence on collision, and `sx` wins over a caller `style`.
